### Post by cdenley on 2010-03-19
You just want to clear the rules?
```

sudo iptables -P INPUT ACCEPT
sudo iptables -F

```
I'm guessing you overlooked that flushing the rules doesn't set the default policy for all chains to ACCEPT. With the default policy for INPUT set to DROP, flushing the rules would make it impossible to connect to the server.

---
